prompt: Luis Suárez was called 'the saviour of Uruguay' for his goal-line handball against Ghana at the 2010 World Cup.
explanation: He was sent off, but Gyan missed the resulting penalty and Uruguay won the shootout to reach the semi-final.

prompt: Dennis Bergkamp famously refused to fly for almost his entire career.
explanation: Bergkamp had a fear of flying after an in-flight incident in 1994 and would travel by train/car wherever possible.

prompt: The Italian national football team's iconic blue shirt is in honour of which historical institution?
explanation: The blue (azzurro) is the official colour of Italy's royal House of Savoy.

prompt: Petr Čech wore a protective head guard after a serious skull fracture sustained against Reading in 2006.
explanation: Čech wore the rugby-style helmet for the rest of his career.

prompt: Zlatan Ibrahimović once turned down a trial at Arsenal saying 'Zlatan does not do auditions'.
explanation: Wenger suggested a trial; Zlatan walked out.

prompt: Which Ireland captain walked out of the 2002 World Cup squad after a row with manager Mick McCarthy in Saipan?
explanation: Roy Keane's exit from the Ireland camp in Saipan became one of football's most famous bust-ups.

prompt: Ronaldinho received a standing ovation from Real Madrid fans after a 3-0 Barcelona win at the Bernabéu in 2005.
explanation: Real Madrid fans applauded his two-goal performance — a rare cross-rivalry honour.

clues: Argentine striker, born 1984.
Won the Copa Libertadores and Intercontinental Cup with Boca Juniors.
Played for Corinthians, West Ham, Manchester United and Manchester City.
Refused to come on as a sub against Bayern Munich in 2011.
Returned to Boca Juniors after a spell in China.
explanation: Carlos Tevez: relentless, controversial, beloved by his clubs.

prompt: On 13 May 2012, against which side did Sergio Agüero score his iconic title-winning goal?
explanation: City beat QPR 3-2 with Agüero scoring in the 93rd minute — '93:20'.

prompt: Bookmakers had Leicester at 5000-1 to win the Premier League before the 2015-16 season.
explanation: Leicester's title win is the most famous defied-odds story in football history.

clues: Portuguese manager, born 1963.
Won the UEFA Cup with Porto in 2003.
Famously poked Tito Vilanova in the eye.
Has managed Chelsea twice — among many other clubs.
Coined the phrase 'I'm a special one' at his Chelsea unveiling in 2004.
explanation: José Mourinho: serial trophy winner across Europe.

prompt: Jamie Vardy broke the Premier League record by scoring in how many consecutive matches in 2015?
explanation: Vardy passed Ruud van Nistelrooy's 10-game record.

prompt: South Korea's 2002 World Cup run was marred by controversial refereeing in their wins against which two European sides?
explanation: South Korea beat Italy in the round of 16 and Spain in the quarter-final in heavily contested matches.

prompt: Kylian Mbappé became the second teenager after Pelé to score in a World Cup Final, in 2018.
explanation: Mbappé was 19 when he scored against Croatia in the 4-2 final win.

prompt: Whose Panenka penalty broke English hearts in the Euro 2012 quarter-final shootout?
explanation: Pirlo's cheeky chip helped Italy eliminate England.

prompt: The 2020 Champions League final was played in Lisbon as a single-leg tournament due to COVID-19.
explanation: UEFA condensed the latter knockout rounds into one-leg ties in Lisbon's Estádio da Luz.

prompt: At which stadium did Zinedine Zidane score his iconic 2002 Champions League Final volley?
explanation: Zidane's volley against Bayer Leverkusen at Hampden Park is widely regarded as the greatest UCL final goal.

prompt: Manchester City fans popularised 'The Poznań' celebration — turning their backs and bouncing — after a Europa League trip in 2010.
explanation: Fans adopted it after seeing Lech Poznań supporters do it at the Etihad.

prompt: Who did Luis Suárez bite at the 2014 World Cup, leading to a 4-month ban?
explanation: Suárez bit Chiellini during a group game; he had previously bitten Otman Bakkal and Branislav Ivanović.

prompt: Andrés Iniesta revealed a t-shirt with 'Dani Jarque siempre con nosotros' after his 2010 World Cup-winning goal.
explanation: The tribute was to his friend Dani Jarque, Espanyol's captain who had died the previous year.

prompt: Jens Lehmann was sent off in the first half of the 2006 Champions League Final.
explanation: Lehmann was dismissed for fouling Eto'o; Arsenal led but Barcelona came back to win 2-1.

prompt: Erling Haaland scored a hat-trick in his second Premier League home appearance for Manchester City.
explanation: Haaland scored hat-tricks in three consecutive home matches early in 2022-23.

prompt: Tottenham reached the 2019 Champions League Final under Mauricio Pochettino.
explanation: Spurs lost 2-0 to Liverpool in Madrid after a famous comeback against Ajax in the semi.

prompt: Luka Modrić's 2018 Ballon d'Or ended a 10-year streak shared by Messi and Ronaldo.
explanation: Modrić was the first non-Messi/Ronaldo winner since Kaká in 2007.

prompt: Arjen Robben always cut inside onto his left foot — it never worked.
explanation: Everyone knew he was going to do it. He scored a lot of goals doing it anyway.

prompt: Who managed Villarreal to the 2021 Europa League title against Manchester United?
explanation: Emery's Villarreal won 11-10 on penalties — David de Gea missed the decisive spot kick.

prompt: Paul the Octopus correctly predicted the result of all seven Germany matches plus the 2010 World Cup Final.
explanation: Paul went 8-for-8, becoming a global cultural phenomenon.

prompt: Greece's Euro 2004 winning manager was a German.
explanation: Otto Rehhagel led Greece to one of football's biggest upsets.

prompt: José Mourinho poked Barcelona's assistant Tito Vilanova in the eye during a 2011 Spanish Super Cup brawl.
explanation: The incident at Camp Nou became one of the most infamous moments of the Real Madrid-Barcelona rivalry.

prompt: Pep Guardiola won the Champions League with Bayern Munich during his three years there.
explanation: Pep won three Bundesligas with Bayern but never the UCL — losing in the semi-finals each year.

prompt: Radamel Falcao joined Manchester United on loan in 2014 from which club?
explanation: Falcao moved to Monaco from Atlético Madrid in 2013, then went on loan to United and later Chelsea.

prompt: Wayne Rooney's iconic overhead kick was scored in a Manchester derby in February 2011.
explanation: Rooney's bicycle kick beat Manchester City 2-1 at Old Trafford and won Goal of the Decade.

prompt: José Mourinho sprinted across the Camp Nou pitch celebrating after Inter eliminated Barcelona in the 2010 UCL semi-final.
explanation: Mourinho's celebration became iconic; Inter went on to complete the treble.

prompt: Which club's 2017-18 Premier League title-winning team is nicknamed 'The Centurions'?
explanation: Manchester City reached 100 points — a Premier League first.

prompt: Zinedine Zidane's last act as a footballer was a headbutt on Marco Materazzi in the 2006 World Cup Final.
explanation: Zidane was sent off in extra time; Italy went on to win on penalties.

prompt: Portsmouth won the 2008 FA Cup beating Cardiff City in the final.
explanation: Portsmouth won 1-0 at the new Wembley with a Kanu goal.

prompt: Liverpool finished 2nd in the 2013-14 Premier League, two points behind Manchester City.
explanation: Steven Gerrard's slip against Chelsea is most associated with that title race collapse.

prompt: How many consecutive Premier League games did Arsenal go unbeaten during their Invincibles run?
explanation: Arsenal's 49-game unbeaten run finally ended at Old Trafford in October 2004.

prompt: Eric Cantona's famous 'When the seagulls follow the trawler' quote came after his kung-fu kick incident.
explanation: Delivered at a 1995 press conference about Selhurst Park; among football's most quoted lines.

clues: Italian playmaker, born 1976.
Spent his entire 25-year senior career at one club.
Won the 2006 World Cup with Italy.
Roma's all-time top scorer.
Wore the number 10 shirt his whole career.
explanation: Totti: the eternal captain of Roma.

prompt: Luka Modrić joined Real Madrid in 2012 from which Premier League club?
explanation: Modrić moved from Spurs to Real Madrid in a £30m deal.

prompt: Nottingham Forest have won more European Cups than they have League titles.
explanation: Forest won 2 European Cups (1979, 1980) and 1 league title (1978).

prompt: Tottenham won the 2008 League Cup beating Chelsea in the final.
explanation: Spurs won 2-1 at Wembley; it remains their most recent major trophy as of 2024.

prompt: Richarlison's celebration after his bicycle-kick goal at the 2022 World Cup is known as 'the pigeon dance'.
explanation: He performed it after scoring against Serbia in the group stage.

prompt: Roman Abramovich bought which Premier League club in 2003?
explanation: Abramovich's purchase of Chelsea kicked off the modern era of oligarch-owned clubs.

prompt: Pep Guardiola's Barcelona won six trophies in the 2009 calendar year.
explanation: La Liga, Copa del Rey, UCL, Spanish Super Cup, UEFA Super Cup, Club World Cup.

prompt: Newcastle United were taken over by a Saudi-led consortium in which year?
explanation: The Public Investment Fund-led takeover completed in October 2021, ending the Mike Ashley era.

prompt: Kevin De Bruyne joined Manchester City in 2015 from which club?
explanation: De Bruyne moved from Wolfsburg to City for £55m after being sold by Chelsea two years earlier.

prompt: Jude Bellingham joined Real Madrid from Borussia Dortmund in 2023.
explanation: Bellingham moved for around €103m and took the iconic number 5 shirt.

prompt: Kylian Mbappé joined Real Madrid from PSG on a free transfer in 2024.
explanation: Mbappé moved to Real Madrid on a free after his PSG contract expired in summer 2024.

prompt: Vinícius Júnior joined Real Madrid as a teenager from Flamengo.
explanation: Vinícius signed for Real Madrid in 2017 but stayed at Flamengo until he turned 18 in 2018.

clues: Argentine striker, born 1988.
Joined Manchester City from Atlético Madrid in 2011.
Scored the most famous goal in Premier League history in May 2012.
Manchester City's all-time top scorer.
Retired in 2021 after a brief spell at Barcelona.
explanation: Sergio Agüero: City legend, 93:20 forever.

prompt: Steve McClaren was sacked as England manager after failing to qualify for Euro 2008.
explanation: McClaren earned the 'Wally with the Brolly' nickname after the 3-2 home defeat to Croatia.

prompt: What was Juventus's punishment after the 2006 Calciopoli scandal?
correct: Relegated to Serie B and stripped of two Serie A titles
distractors: Banned from Europe for two years only
Relegated to Serie C and stripped of three titles
explanation: Juventus were relegated to Serie B (2006-07), docked points, and lost the 2004-05 and 2005-06 titles.

prompt: Cristiano Ronaldo wore the number 7 shirt on his Manchester United debut in 2003.
explanation: He inherited the number from David Beckham, against Bolton at Old Trafford.

prompt: Rodri became the first Manchester City player to win the Ballon d'Or in 2024.
explanation: Rodri pipped Vinícius Júnior in a controversial vote.

prompt: Fernando Torres scored the winning goal in the Euro 2008 Final.
explanation: Torres beat Philipp Lahm and lobbed Jens Lehmann for Spain's first major title since 1964.

prompt: Erling Haaland set a new single-season Premier League goalscoring record in 2022-23 with 36 goals.
explanation: Haaland broke Alan Shearer and Andrew Cole's 34-goal record (set when seasons were 42 games).

prompt: What was the half-time score in the 2005 Champions League Final between Liverpool and AC Milan?
explanation: Liverpool came back from 3-0 down to win on penalties — the 'Miracle of Istanbul'.

prompt: David de Gea made 14 saves in Manchester United's 2-1 win at Arsenal in December 2017 — a Premier League record.
explanation: De Gea's 14 saves matched the Premier League record at the time.

prompt: Andrea Pirlo joined Juventus on a free transfer from AC Milan in 2011.
explanation: Milan let him go on a free; Pirlo went on to win four Serie A titles with Juve.

prompt: Luis Suárez was the fastest player ever to reach 100 La Liga goals at the time.
explanation: Cristiano Ronaldo currently holds the record for fastest to 100 La Liga goals.

prompt: Wigan Athletic won the FA Cup and were relegated from the Premier League in the same season.
explanation: Wigan beat Manchester City 1-0 in the 2013 final via a Ben Watson header, then went down.

prompt: Who was Chelsea's interim manager when they won the 2012 Champions League?
explanation: Di Matteo took over from AVB and led Chelsea to a shock UCL triumph.

prompt: Kylian Mbappé broke Thierry Henry's Monaco youngest goalscorer record.
explanation: Mbappé broke Henry's record as Monaco's youngest goalscorer at 17.

prompt: Thierry Henry is Arsenal's all-time top scorer.
explanation: Henry overtook Ian Wright in 2005 and finished his Arsenal career with 228 goals.

prompt: Mario Balotelli reportedly set off fireworks in his bathroom in 2011, leading to a fire.
explanation: The night before the famous Manchester derby in which he scored twice and revealed his 'Why Always Me?' shirt.

prompt: Luka Modrić won the 2018 World Cup Golden Ball despite Croatia losing the final.
explanation: Modrić was named Player of the Tournament after captaining Croatia to the final.

prompt: Who scored Real Madrid's winning goal in the 2022 Champions League Final against Liverpool?
explanation: Vinícius scored the only goal in Paris as Real won their 14th European Cup.

prompt: Manchester City won the treble in 2022-23.
explanation: Premier League, FA Cup and Champions League — only the second English club after Manchester United (1999).

prompt: Bayer Leverkusen went the entire 2023-24 Bundesliga season unbeaten.
explanation: Under Xabi Alonso, Leverkusen won their first ever Bundesliga title without losing a league match.

prompt: Bukayo Saka missed the decisive penalty in the Euro 2020 Final shootout.
explanation: Saka's miss after Sancho's and Rashford's gave Italy the title at Wembley.

prompt: Lamine Yamal became the youngest goalscorer in European Championship history at Euro 2024.
explanation: Yamal was 16 when he scored in Spain's semi-final win over France.
